title: adhatoda incana
intro: Adhatoda incana, also known as Vasa or Vasaka, is a plant native to India and other parts of Asia, belonging to the family Acanthaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Adhatoda incana include treating respiratory issues such as asthma, bronchitis, and coughs, as well as reducing inflammation and improving digestion. Herbal preparations of Adhatoda incana include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be made by boiling the leaves in water, and are often used to treat a variety of health conditions.
